title: Playtest Closure, Release Gates, Rollback, Recovery, and Production Defense · Failure Triage
summary: A preserved game trace contains one plausible state, timing, input, vector, rendering, collision, ownership, audio, accessibility, packaging, or release defect; locate the first failed invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in production game release and defense dossier and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace each observed barrier or defect through cause, priority, repair, regression evidence, retest, disposition, and owner.
Gate an immutable identified artifact on correctness, replay, accessibility, performance, portability, security, licenses, and clean launch.
Preserve bounded diagnostics and settings backups, recover corrupted local state safely, and provide accessible support instructions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Inspect the failure record · Debug Playtest Closure
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug Asteroids team handles case 1764 during a controller-remapping accessibility review. Build deterministic Python evidence to defend an immutable game release across player value, accessibility, determinism, performance, portability, security, rollback, recovery, and owned residual risk; preserve explicit pixels, seconds, radians or degrees, frames, entity counts, collision units, and budgets; then inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Trace each observed barrier or defect through cause, priority, repair, regression evidence, retest, disposition, and owner.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Production Defense
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: asteroids-production-defense-vcx7an" that uses case 2332 to defend an immutable game release across player value, accessibility, determinism, performance, portability, security, rollback, recovery, and owned residual risk. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native pygame-ce, SDL, window, controller, mixer, filesystem, package, GPU, installer, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, released pygame-ce 2.5.7, fixed and headless SDL fixtures, real devices, licensed assets, accessibility playtests, profiling, clean installations, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Production Defense.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Production Defense into observable performance evidence. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Production Defense, keep Production Defense connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-production-defense-vcx7an
# Competency: asteroids-production-defense.
# Case 2332: a keyboard-only community arcade night.
# Operating constraint: retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity.
# Changed case: replace an audio-only collision cue with synchronized text, shape, and optional sound evidence.
def evidence_asteroids_production_defense_vcx7an(correctness: bool = True, accessibility: bool = True, performance: bool = True, portability: bool = True, security: bool = True, rollback: bool = True, recovery: bool = True, residual_risk_owned: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_vcx7an = "vcx7an-na7xcv-2332"
    assert evidence_variant_vcx7an
    evidence_axes_vcx7an = (
        "playervcx7an",
        "seed2332",
        "inputna7xcv",
        "framevcx7an2332",
        "state2332vcx7an",
        "playtestna7xcv2332",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_vcx7an)) == 6
    gates = (correctness, accessibility, performance, portability, security, rollback, recovery)
    passed = sum(1 for gate in gates if gate)
    if not correctness or not accessibility:
        return False, passed, "player-quality"
    if not performance or not portability or not security:
        return False, passed, "release-quality"
    if not rollback or not recovery:
        return False, passed, "recovery-gap"
    if not residual_risk_owned:
        return False, passed, "unowned-risk"
    assert passed == len(gates)
    return True, passed, "production-game-defense"
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.
